title: Condaminea corymbosa
taxonomy: Condaminea corymbosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a part of the tribe Spermacoceae, which is a group of flowering plants known for their small, usually hairy, leaves and showy flowers. Condaminea corymbosa is native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in the countries of Colombia and Ecuador. The plant is often found growing in woodland edges and shaded areas, where it can thrive in the humid and warm climate. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Condaminea corymbosa is closely related to other plants such as coffee, gardenias, and jasmine.
